David, we are halfway through the course now. Can you believe it?
Time flies! So tell me, how do you feel about your English now?
Honestly, much better than before. The first weeks were a real challenge.
I remember. What was the biggest turning point for you?
My first full conversation. It was short, but it was unforgettable.
That is a wonderful milestone. And what is still hard for you?
Fast conversations are still a challenge, but I practice every day.
You have really improved. I am sure the second half will be even better.
Learning English has been a long journey for me.
At first it was a real challenge, but I did not give up.
My English is much better than before.
My first conversation was an unforgettable milestone.
I still have a lot to achieve, and I am ready.

When I started learning English, everything felt like a challenge. My old routine had no time for study, so I changed my habits and made English a part of every day. My first real conversation was short, but it was unforgettable. After that, I slowly began to overcome my fear of making mistakes. The biggest turning point was simple: I stopped worrying about being perfect. Today my English is much better than before. I can talk about my past, my neighborhood, and my plans. I still have a lot to achieve, but every small milestone makes me proud.

Hi Ana Paula, it is Sarah. I just wanted to say how proud I am of your progress. A few months ago, speaking English was a real challenge for you, and now look at you. You describe your neighborhood, you tell stories about your past, and you compare things with so much more confidence. Your pronunciation has improved a lot, and you are much faster than before. I know fast conversations are still hard, but that is completely normal. You have reached an important milestone. Keep going, and enjoy the second half of the journey. Bye!

Learning English has been a long journey. At first, speaking was a real challenge. Then I changed my daily routine and studied every morning. My first full conversation was an unforgettable milestone. Now my English is much better than before.
